content:
Spotify launched their codes in ~2019 and now there are many services to leverage this feature, enabling you to get a sticker of your favorite songs code among other products like key fobs, mugs, shirts.
The code can be scanned by the Spotify App and will play the scanned song / album / artist.
I’ve seen posts on social media where people would print an albums cover with the corresponding code and hang it onto their walls. I loved the idea and started with a first batch until I discovered that you couldn’t simply drag-and-drop the album cover plus code into your file explorer to get the image. It wouldn’t appear anywhere in the desktop app and I’ve searched for a long time until I decided to make this a friday night project.
Components I’ve decided to do this in Python due to the sheer amount of packages already present.
There are three objectives here:
 Get the album cover through the Spotify API Get the corresponding code Merge it all together  Album Cover SpotiPy uses the two environment variables for accessing the API - after that it was quite easy to grab the album cover:
results = spotify.album(album_uri) link_to_cover = results["images"][0]["url"] cover_size = results["images"][0]["height"] Results are already sorted from highest to lowest dimensions.
Spotify Code Spotify has a website up to download codes only, providing options for background colors, bar color, output format and size.
I discovered that the download link was parameterized (bottom line):
The covers I’ve seen always matched the “average” color of the album art. For this I used colorthief; the bar color would be picked depending on the mean RGB of the average colour. Size is determined by the album cover size. I plug those values into the URL and use PIL to download the picture.
Putting it all together PIL turned out to be a good option to easily merge two pictures and was a pleasure to work with.
After doing this I’ve added argparse for more robust CLI-behaviour.
 Revisiting this the hardest part was trying to mitigate downloading the pictures to temp. I’ve found this snipped on StackOverflow where somebody came up with a clever solution which I adjusted for my usecase:
add snippet here

title: Bring Back Spotify Album Codes!
